descriptions.value: Infantry unit, second class - Sniper rifle.
Metal.
Crrystal.
Crystal.
Heavy Tank Destroyer; effective against infantry and vehicle pilots. Ground only.
Anti-gravity platform, equipped with a plasma beam cannon. Only ground targets.
Fast and agile fighter jet; requires a Fighterstation.
Equipped with an Anti-Missile Defense System and reflective armor (effective against energy beam and energy impulse weapons).
Equipped with the powerful engine MKIII and two rocket launchers.
Jaguar units are larger and more heavily armed as the Kazuar units. Ground only.
Medium-range assault aircraft.
Repair unit, can be mounted on the majority of all other units.
No armament, only for unit transport.
The only aircraft against air and ground targets; by default armed with chaingun.

Zombies are everywhere in Antaloor. They’re normally creatures of the night, but the advance of the Taint seems to have  encouraged them to venture out into the bright light of day – they congregate mainly in cemeteries, where they lurk in wait with their bony allies, the Skeletons.

descriptions.name: Zombie
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: This youngster is cannon fodder for most heroes – his attack and defense values are still minimal – so if you meet one, it’s a good opportunity to boost your inventory!

descriptions.name: Young Grom
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: Try to find the secret entrance to the burial chambers - the cave entrance is located to the southeast of the temple, behind a small hill. Descend into the cave and follow the path... until Whitehead himself suddenly stands before you!

descriptions.name: Whitehead
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: The dungeons is where you'll meet these bony warriors - and they'll usually attack you in groups.

descriptions.name: Skeleton
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: This is the most widespread monster in Antaloor. Whether in mountain forests or in the far-off, treeless, arid zone to the south, the Reapers are everywhere.

descriptions.name: Reaper
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: The Orcs occupy the entire area south of the Gon. Their capital city is Gor Gammar, in the heart of the lava plains. Orc warriors are feared by all because of their enormous strength and their battle discipline.

descriptions.name: Orc
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: Evolution branched off a little when it came to the Master Serpents - they're even more dangerous than their cousins. Highly intelligent, they can strike you with awesome power.

descriptions.name: Master Serpent
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: The Mantis is an enormous version of that merciless killer, the Praying Mantis. An extremely strong layer of chitin gives it almost 100% protection from arrows and light sword blows.

descriptions.name: Mantis
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: Kyra, the hero's twin sister, disappears in circumstances that can only be described as mysterious.

descriptions.name: Kyra (Trading Card)
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: The hero, a young bounty hunter, travels throughout Antaloor searching for his sister Kyra. His quests take him to the far-off reaches of the continent.

descriptions.name: Knight (Trading Card)
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: These usually lead the Hell Warriors. Extremely strong bone armor and horns make them much more resistant to blows than the normal Hell Warriors, so they’re more dangerous to your health in an encounter.

descriptions.name: Hell Master
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value:
Dragons are - without a doubt - the most impressive creatures on the entire continent. In addition to the powerful Alpha Dragons, many species of Dwarf Dragons have also evolved - but don't start thinking, "Hah!
Dwarf Dragon? No problem for me"... this "Dwarf" Dragon is a lot bigger than you think - in fact, it's bigger than any human on Antaloor!

descriptions.name: Dwarf Dragon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value:
Northern Thalmont borders the Dwarves' territory - and it's a wild region. The law of the
wilderness rules here. Packs of wolves and bears are all over the place. Brotherhood
outposts are the last, lonely signs of civilization.

descriptions.name: Dwarf
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value: Demons really live up to their reputation - among their ranks, you'll find some of the most dangerous creatures in all of Antaloor.

descriptions.name: Demon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value:
Your adventure starts in Antaloor's far north, in the mountainous region of Thalmont. A
mysterious letter, which was the first clue of your twin sister Kyra's whereabouts for many
months, has led you to the small village of Komorin.

descriptions.name: Bounty Hunter
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card
